runs.content:
Otis Worldwide picked up in Portfolio 1 at the previous close as a wide-moat, Service-segment-driven industrial trading near its 52-week low, a ~31% discount to Morningstar's $105 fair value estimate, amid a broad Materials/Financials/Discretionary sell-off.

AZZ Inc entered Portfolio 2 at today's open (+0.38% gap) after a Q1 FY2027 EPS beat and raised FY26 guidance drove a genuine ~2.39x average-volume spike, confirmed by computing a 20-day average directly from price history after two other candidates (MXL, WDFC) failed to clear the 2x bar.

Penguin Solutions picked up in Portfolio 1 at the previous close after a Q3 FY26 double beat (revenue +47.6% YoY) and raised guidance (22% vs prior ~12%) drove a +25.1% session on ~4.0x average volume.

Same name entered in Portfolio 2 at today's open (+5.26% gap from prior close) to test whether the volume-spike momentum survives an overnight gap, alongside the same AI/semiconductor tailwind lifting the sector +4.5% despite fresh US-Iran airstrikes.
